steps.title: Connect error to naming
steps.content: The student's goal is to emphasize the role a misconception played in the naming of a place. The relevant notes contain two necessary pieces: Spanish explorers thought the land off Mexico's west coast was an island, and they named it California after the fictional island in Las sergas de Esplandián. A successful choice must make the error clear, identify the place as the peninsula now known as Baja California, and connect that mistaken belief to the resulting name.

steps.title: Require cause and result
steps.content: Choice A mentions only the fictional island in the novel, so it does not explain the naming of a real place. Choice B says Baja California was originally named after a fictional place, but it leaves out the mistaken belief that the peninsula was an island. Choice C says explorers named a peninsula California, but it gives no reason why that name followed from an error. Choice D states that explorers were thinking it was an island when they named a peninsula after an island from a popular novel, so both the misconception and the naming are present. The answer is D.
